name: Izki Btt Center Route 8 - circular from Antoñana
name_original: Centro Btt Izki Ruta 8 – circular desde Antoñana
region_intro: Mountain biking around Campezo / Kanpezu offers diverse landscapes within the province of Álava in the Basque Country. The region is characterized by several mountain ranges, including the Sierra de Codes and Sierra de Loquiz, with peaks like Joar Summit reaching over 1,400 meters. Riders can explore varied terrain, from the Ega River valley to dense forests of beeches and oaks, and the unique geological formations of Izki Natural Park. This area provides a network of trails suitable for various skill levels, from established BTT Centers to dedicated downhill lines. Best mountain bike trails around Campezo / Kanpezu The most popular mountain bike trail is Centro Btt Izki Ruta 6 – Vuelta de Parque Natural de Izkiz, a 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the diverse landscapes of Izki Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Izki Btt Center Route 8 - circular from Antoñana, a moderate 17.5 miles (28.2 km) path. This circular route offers scenic views and varied terrain around Antoñana. Local mountain bikers also love the Santa Cruz de Campezo – Bitigarra loop from Santa Cruz de Campezo / Santikurutze Kanpezu, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail leading through mountain foothills and forests, often completed in about 6 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Campezo / Kanpezu is defined by mountain ranges, river valleys, and diverse forests, including unique beech woods. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging ascents. The routes in Campezo / Kanpezu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 8 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campezo / Kanpezu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails in Campezo / Kanpezu, offering a mix of moderate and difficult routes. The region features established BTT Centers and dedicated downhill lines, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campezo / Kanpezu are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain peaks, and the well-maintained network of trails that explore dense forests and unique geological formations.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for families in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: While the listed routes tend towards moderate and difficult, the region does offer options for less experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Campezo/Kanpezu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del FF.CC Vasco–Navarro&lt;/a&gt;, a historic 'greenway', provides a flatter, more touring-friendly cycling experience that can be adapted for families. For specific easy routes, it's best to check local BTT Centers for their family-friendly recommendations.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, Campezo / Kanpezu offers several challenging routes for advanced riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10524031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cruz de Campezo – Bitigarra loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 60.7 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15005090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m) – Ibernalo Hermitage loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the highest point in the Codés mountain range.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Campezo / Kanpezu are circular. A popular moderate circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056839554/izki-btt-center-route-8-circular-from-antonana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Izki Btt Center Route 8 - circular from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt;, covering 28.2 km. For a more challenging circular ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bujanda Village – Mojon Alto Trail Junction loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by waterfalls in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: Yes, the region around Campezo / Kanpezu features several waterfalls that can be explored. While not directly on the listed mountain bike routes, you can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igoroin Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4387004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabando Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; nearby, which might be accessible via short detours or separate hiking paths.

Q: What kind of scenic viewpoints can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campezo / Kanpezu offer diverse scenic viewpoints. You can expect panoramic views from peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1255178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m)&lt;/a&gt;, especially on routes that ascend the Codés mountain range. The trails also wind through beautiful forests of beeches and oaks, and offer vistas of river valleys and unique geological formations within the Izki Natural Park.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or landmarks to explore by bike?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, the region boasts several natural features. The Izki Natural Park offers diverse landscapes, and the impressive limestone tower of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1254551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de los Cencerros&lt;/a&gt; (Sleeping Lion Peak) is a notable landmark. You'll also encounter rich forests, including the southernmost beech forests in Navarre near Joar Peak, and the Ega River valley.

Q: Is there public transport available to Santa Cruz de Campezo?
A: Santa Cruz de Campezo, also known as Santikurutze Kanpezu, is accessible by bus. While specific routes and schedules can vary, local bus services connect the village to larger towns in the Álava province. It's advisable to check current bus timetables for the most up-to-date information on connections to Santa Cruz de Campezo.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: Parking is generally available in Santa Cruz de Campezo and other starting points for mountain bike routes. Many BTT Centers (Mountain Bike Centers) in the region, such as those associated with Izki, provide dedicated parking facilities for riders. Look for designated parking areas in villages or at trailheads.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: The best season for mountain biking in Campezo / Kanpezu is typically spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter riding is possible, but conditions can be challenging, especially at higher elevations, due to potential snow or ice.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike trails in Campezo / Kanpezu?
A: While specific dog-friendly mountain bike trails are not explicitly designated, many natural trails in the region are generally accessible for dogs, provided they are well-behaved and kept under control. Always ensure your dog is capable of the distance and terrain, and respect local regulations regarding pets in natural parks or protected areas. It's recommended to check with local information centers for any specific restrictions.
tips: Right off the main trail is a smaller trail that takes you up to the dam. You can see it through the trees from above on the main trail. It's a small dam but creates a nice little reservoir.
This trail can get overgrown when it's not used much, but it's well marked so it's pretty easy to follow. It's tight and fun like singletrack should be.
There are five trails that come together here. Many of them turn around and return to this place. The area is really nice and shady, but it can be confusing if you're not sure which trail to take.
